news.title: NGT refuses to stay Telangana government's Future City project, calls for counters
news.para: The National Green Tribunal's southern zone bench refused to halt the Telangana government's Future City Project, directing authorities to file responses. An environmentalist had petitioned, alleging development began without mandatory environmental clearances. The tribunal adjourned the case for further hearing on June 9.

news.title: Haryana: After HC stay on S+4, town planning asks HSVP how infrastructure funds were used
news.para: Haryana has paused all stilt-plus-four construction approvals. This follows a court stay and concerns over infrastructure capacity. The town and country planning department is now demanding a report on how 689 crore rupees were spent on upgrades. This money was meant for infrastructure linked to increased density. The court highlighted the strain on services like sewage and traffic.

news.title: Ghaziabad: GDA makes rooftop solar panels space in building maps must for approval
news.para: Ghaziabad mandates rooftop solar panel space in building maps, rejecting non-compliant submissions. A refundable fee, tiered by plot size, is required for map approval under the PM Surya Ghar scheme. This initiative aims to make Ghaziabad a solar city, targeting 10% renewable energy by 2027.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC to auction prime Worli plot at ₹455 crore
news.para: Weeks after an inquiry was ordered into a Rs 600-crore tender, the BMC has now floated a new tender to auction a prime 5,166sqm plot for commercial exploitation at a base price of Rs 455 crore. The highest bidder can construct residential or commercial towers, with the developer also required to provide office space for the BMC's testing laboratory.

news.title: Mumbai developers pour ₹11,626 crore into BMC coffers in FY26
news.para: Developers emerged as the BMC's top revenue source in 2025-26, contributing Rs 11,626 crore through premiums and development charges, significantly exceeding property tax collections. This surge is attributed to extensive construction, particularly building redevelopment, with areas like Bandra-Jogeshwari leading revenue generation.

news.title: CCI clears Indriya's 14.3% stake acquisition in Aditya Birla Housing Finance
news.para: The Competition Commission of India has officially sanctioned Indriya's foray into Aditya Birla Housing Finance, paving the way for a landmark ₹2,750 crore investment. Through this deal, Indriya, already a part of Advent International, will capture a 14.29% ownership, leaving Aditya Birla Capital firmly in control of the majority stake in its housing finance operations.

news.title: Embassy REIT approves ₹1,100 crore commercial paper issuance
news.para: Embassy REIT is set to secure ₹1,100 crore via commercial papers, a strategic initiative aimed at refinancing existing obligations and bolstering working capital. This issuance will be executed in two batches, with durations of 347 and 342 days, forming a crucial segment of their ambitious ₹10,500 crore fundraising goal.

news.title: CCI clears Warburg Pincus' stake buy in Fleur Hotels; approves Lemon Tree restructuring
news.para: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has officially sanctioned Warburg Pincus' stake acquisition in Fleur Hotels, part of the Lemon Tree Hotels umbrella. With this nod of approval, the Lemon Tree group is set for an internal transformation, fostering two distinct avenues for expansion.

news.title: Government to soon restore conversion of DDA properties from leasehold to freehold: Housing minister
news.para: Property owners in Delhi can soon convert their leasehold properties to freehold. Union minister Manohar Lal stated that conversion rates are being finalized. This will allow the process to resume after a halt. New circle rates announced by the Delhi government will be applicable.

news.title: NCLT approves merger of Nirmal Lifestyle Realty with Oberoi Realty
news.para: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved the merger of Nirmal Lifestyle Realty with Oberoi Realty. This move will simplify the group's structure and consolidate real estate operations. Nirmal Lifestyle Realty will be dissolved without winding up. Oberoi Realty will absorb all assets and liabilities.

news.title: Nexus Select Trust acquires 2.44 lakh sq ft mall in Kolkata for nearly ₹350 crore
news.para: Nexus Select Trust has bought Diamond Plaza in Kolkata for Rs 347.5 crore. This shopping mall offers about 2,44,000 sq ft of leasable area. The deal is expected to conclude within the next four months.

news.title: Centre to regularise 1,511 unauthorised colonies in Delhi on ‘as-is where-is’ basis
news.para: The central government has approved regularising 1,511 unauthorised colonies in Delhi. This move under the PM-UDAY framework allows residents to get legal recognition for their properties without approved layout plans. The decision aims to resolve long-standing issues related to property rights and infrastructure access.  The application process begins April 24.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh CM launches projects in Agra worth Rs 6,466 crore, says 'Greater Agra' township to come up
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath inaugurated development projects worth over Rs 6,400 crore in Agra. He announced plans for a new 'Greater Agra' township, aiming to create a major urban and economic hub. This ambitious project will offer housing and modern amenities for thousands of families.

news.title: Centre notifies revised TOD rules for Delhi, opens 207 sq km along metro corridors for high-density housing
news.para: New rules for Transit Oriented Development in Delhi are now in effect. These regulations aim to build more homes and businesses near metro and transit lines. The policy will cover a large area, including new zones for development. Developers can build more on larger plots, with a focus on affordable housing. A single window clearance will speed up approvals.

news.title: NGT admits plea against sand mining clearance in Odisha's Mayurbhanj
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has admitted an appeal challenging an environmental clearance for a sand mining project in Odisha's Mayurbhanj district. The clearance, granted by SEIAA, is contested due to alleged procedural lapses, including a lack of mandatory site inspection and non-consideration of objections. The NGT will hear the case further on May 13.

news.title: Arvind SmartSpaces to redevelop residential project in Goregaon West, Mumbai
news.para: Arvind SmartSpaces is set to develop a major residential project in Goregaon (West), Mumbai, with an impressive revenue potential of ₹2,400 crore. This significant venture, in partnership with Sigma Oxford Realtors, will feature a substantial saleable area and marks the company's growing footprint in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.

news.title: Ahmedabad: Three construction sites in Gota sealed for not installing green nets
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation has sealed three major construction sites in Gota for failing to install mandatory green nets and barricades. Additionally, a ₹1 lakh fine was imposed on one site for material spillage onto public roads, while other sites faced penalties for pollution and safety violations. Encroachments were also cleared to improve traffic flow.

news.title: Savills acquires majority stake in Hotelivate to expand hospitality advisory in APAC
news.para: Global real estate firm Savills has acquired a majority stake in South Asia-focused Hotelivate, strengthening its hospitality real estate presence in the Asia-Pacific region. The combined entity, 'Hotelivate-Savills,' will leverage Hotelivate's expertise and Savills' global platform to capitalize on growing investor interest in hospitality assets. This strategic move enhances Savills' specialist capabilities in key markets like South Asia.

news.title: Residents of Secunderabad Cantonment Board seek reduction in transfer of property tax to 1.5%
news.para: Secunderabad Cantonment residents are protesting a 5% property tax rate set in 1989, arguing it's outdated and lacks public consultation. They demand a fresh notification, transparent objection process, and a reduction to 1.5%, aligning with GHMC rates. The Cantonment Vikas Manch highlights the need for fairness and democratic principles in tax imposition.
